title: Hollywood Actor Joins Welsh TV Show After Learning the Language
summary: A Hollywood actor named Hans Obma is joining a Welsh TV show called Pobl y Cwm. He learned Welsh because his grandmother was from Wales. He says Wales feels like his second home now. The show is a soap opera that has been on TV for 51 years. It is mostly in Welsh, but Hans plays an American character so he keeps his American accent. He learned Welsh by watching the show and other Welsh programs. He is very excited to act in Welsh and says it is the most fun he has ever had. His grandmother moved to the US after World War Two, and he visited Wales with her once. He says her Welsh accent came back when she was there. Hans has also been in other famous shows like Better Call Saul and The Vampire Diaries.

keywords.explanation: A TV show that tells stories about people's lives and is on many times a week.
The language spoken in Wales, a country in the United Kingdom.
The way people from a place speak a language.
A place where you feel as comfortable as your own home.

questions.correct_answer: He plays an American and keeps his accent

questions.question: How did Hans Obma learn Welsh?

background_read: Pobl y Cwm is a Welsh-language soap opera that started in 1974. It is the oldest soap on the BBC. The show is set in a fictional village in Wales. Many famous actors have appeared on it.
Article_Structure: WHO: Hans Obma, a Hollywood actor.
WHAT: He is joining the Welsh soap opera Pobl y Cwm after learning Welsh.
WHERE: In the fictional village of Cwmderi, south-west Wales.
WHY: Because his grandmother was Welsh and he wanted to honor her.

perspectives.description: Hans Obma is excited to act in Welsh and feels Wales is his second home.
The show welcomes a Hollywood actor, which might attract more viewers.
